User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user within the LabFlow application, defining their role and system access. This entity stores minimal information needed for role-based access and internal linking, separate from external authentication provider data.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity, typically corresponding to an external authentication system's user ID.

role.description: The assigned role of the user, determining their permissions and capabilities within the application. ('Student', 'Teacher', or 'Admin/Staff')

Channel.title: Channel
Channel.description: Represents a virtual 'channel' or specific laboratory where inventory items are grouped, mimicking a Discord-like interface.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Channel entity.

name.description: The display name of the channel (e.g., '#laboratory-1').

description.description: A brief description of the type of equipment or activities typically found in this channel.

InventoryItem.title: InventoryItem
InventoryItem.description: Represents a specific piece of equipment or material available for borrowing in a laboratory channel.

id.description: Unique identifier for the InventoryItem entity.

name.description: The name of the inventory item (e.g., 'Arduino Uno R3 Kit').

description.description: A detailed description of the item, including specifications or usage notes. Used for AI suggestions.

channelId.description: Reference to the Channel this item belongs to. (Relationship: Channel 1:N InventoryItem)

status.description: The current availability status of the item. ('Available', 'Locked', 'Borrowed', or 'Maintenance')

isLocked.description: Indicates if the item requires an OTP for borrowing.

location.description: The physical location of the item within its designated laboratory/channel (e.g., 'Shelf A, Bin 3').

imageUrl.description: Optional URL to an image of the inventory item.

OneTimePassword.title: OneTimePassword
OneTimePassword.description: Represents a unique, temporary password generated by a teacher to grant a student access to a locked inventory item.

id.description: Unique identifier for the OneTimePassword entity.

inventoryItemId.description: Reference to the InventoryItem for which this OTP grants access. (Relationship: InventoryItem 1:N OneTimePassword)

generatedByUserId.description: Reference to the User (Teacher) who generated this OTP. (Relationship: User 1:N OneTimePassword)

expiresAt.description: The timestamp when this OTP becomes invalid.

isUsed.description: Indicates whether the OTP has been successfully used to unlock an item.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when this OTP was generated.

BorrowingTransaction.title: BorrowingTransaction
BorrowingTransaction.description: Records the complete lifecycle of an item being borrowed by a student, from request to return.

id.description: Unique identifier for the BorrowingTransaction entity.

inventoryItemId.description: Reference to the InventoryItem that is part of this transaction. (Relationship: InventoryItem 1:N BorrowingTransaction)

borrowerUserId.description: Reference to the User (Student) who initiated and is responsible for this borrowing. (Relationship: User 1:N BorrowingTransaction)

checkoutByUserId.description: Reference to the User (Admin/Staff) who finalized the checkout process. (Relationship: User 1:N BorrowingTransaction)

status.description: The current status of the borrowing transaction. ('QR Generated', 'Checked Out', 'Returned', 'Overdue', or 'Cancelled')

qrCodeData.description: The data encoded within the QR code generated for this transaction, used for scanning and checkout.

borrowDate.description: The timestamp when the item was officially checked out.

expectedReturnDate.description: The anticipated date and time for the item's return.

returnDate.description: The actual timestamp when the item was returned.

otpId.description: Optional reference to the OneTimePassword used to authorize this transaction, if the item was locked. (Relationship: OneTimePassword 1:1 BorrowingTransaction)

notes.description: Any additional notes or comments related to the transaction.

definition.description: Stores minimal user profile information. The 'role' field within this document (if present) is for UI display only and is NOT used for authorization. Authentication roles are managed via dedicated role collections.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, corresponding to Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Represents a teacher role. A document exists here if the user is a teacher. This collection is used for explicit role-based authorization checks (existence over content) in security rules. No additional data is needed in this document.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who is assigned the 'Teacher' role.

definition.description: Represents an Admin/Staff role. A document exists here if the user is Admin/Staff. This collection is used for explicit role-based authorization checks (existence over content) in security rules. No additional data is needed in this document.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who is assigned the 'Admin/Staff' role.

definition.description: Stores information about laboratory channels (e.g., #laboratory-1). These channels are accessible for viewing by all authenticated users (students, teachers, staff) and managed by Admin/Staff.

params.name: channelId
params.description: The unique identifier for the channel.

definition.description: Stores specific inventory items associated with a channel. This subcollection structure facilitates querying items within a given channel. Item access is controlled by user roles and item status ('status', 'isLocked') fields directly on the document.

params.name: channelId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent channel this item belongs to.

params.name: inventoryItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for the inventory item.

definition.description: Stores one-time passwords generated by teachers for locked inventory items. Includes 'inventoryItemId' and 'generatedByUserId' as top-level fields for direct authorization checks and efficient lookup by 'code' and 'inventoryItemId'.

params.name: otpId
params.description: The unique identifier for the one-time password.

definition.description: Records the complete lifecycle of item borrowing. This is a top-level collection to allow Admin/Staff to perform QAPs (efficient list operations) across all transactions. Includes 'borrowerUserId', 'checkoutByUserId', 'inventoryItemId', and 'status' as denormalized fields for authorization, specific user queries, and auditing.

params.name: transactionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the borrowing transaction.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore data structure adheres to the core design principles by prioritizing authorization independence, structural segregation, and consistent access modeling. 

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL) & Denormalization Strategy:**
1.  **Role-Based Access Control (DBAC):** User roles ('Teacher', 'Admin/Staff') are segregated into dedicated top-level collections (`/roles_teachers/{userId}`, `/roles_staff/{userId}`). This design allows security rules to check for the *existence* of a document in these collections to determine a user's role (e.g., `exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/roles_teachers/$(request.auth.uid))`) rather than performing a `get()` on the `/users/{userId}` document. This completely eliminates hierarchical authorization dependencies, enabling atomic operations and simplifying rule logic. The `User` entity itself is a placeholder for basic profile information and does not contain authorization-critical role data for external checks. 
2.  **BorrowingTransaction Ownership:** The `BorrowingTransaction` entity includes `borrowerUserId` and `checkoutByUserId` as top-level fields. This denormalization allows students to securely retrieve their own transactions (by checking `request.auth.uid == resource.data.borrowerUserId`) and Admin/Staff to query/manage all transactions without needing `get()` operations on separate user profiles or inventory items for authorization purposes. 
3.  **OneTimePassword Context:** The `OneTimePassword` entity explicitly stores `inventoryItemId` and `generatedByUserId`. This ensures that security rules for OTP validation can directly verify the associated item and the generating teacher's identity (`request.auth.uid == resource.data.generatedByUserId`) without needing to fetch parent documents.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters) & Structural Segregation:**
1.  **Channels and Inventory Items:** Channels are stored in a top-level collection (`/channels/{channelId}`), which makes listing all available channels straightforward for all authenticated users. Inventory items are structured as a subcollection (`/channels/{channelId}/inventory_items/{inventoryItemId}`). This allows for efficient querying of all items within a specific channel (e.g., for the 'Dynamic Inventory Display' feature) and ensures a homogeneous security posture for items within a given channel context. Since channels are globally viewable by students, no complex hierarchical `get()` calls are needed for basic item access.
2.  **Borrowing Transactions:** All `BorrowingTransaction` entities are placed in a flat top-level collection (`/borrowing_transactions/{transactionId}`). This segregation is crucial for the 'Admin/Staff Inventory & Borrowing Management' feature, allowing staff to perform efficient `list` queries across *all* transactions (a QAP). Students can also list their own transactions by querying the `borrowerUserId` field, which is explicitly indexed for performance. This avoids the need for inefficient collection group queries or complex fan-out reads.

Overall, the structure ensures that authorization rules are explicit, independent, and debuggable, supporting scalable list operations and the integrity of ownership and state modeling through clear structural and denormalization strategies.
